verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for you all, that your faith is spoken of throughout the whole world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: An exordium worthy of this blessed spirit, and able to teach all men to offer unto God the firstlings of their good deeds and words, and to render thanks not only for their own, but also for others’ well-doings: which also maketh the soul pure from envy and grudging, and draweth God in a greater measure towards the loving spirit of them that so render thanks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore also elsewhere he says, “Blessed be God and the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, Who hath blessed us with all spiritual blessing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And it is fitting that we render thanks not only when rich, but also when poor, not when in health only, but also when sick, not when we thrive only, but also when we have to bear the reverse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when our affairs are borne onward with a fair wind, to be thankful is not matter of wonder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when no small tempests be upon us, and the vessel veers about and is in jeopardy, then is the great time for displaying patience and goodness of heart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this cause Job also gained a crown from hence, and the shameless mouth of the devil did he stop, and show clearly that not even when he saw good days was it through his wealth that he was thankful, but through his much love toward God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And see too what things he is thankful for: not for things earthly and perishing, as power and authority and glory (for these things are of no account), but for real blessings, faith and boldness of speech.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And with how much feeling he gives thanks: for he saith not “to God,” but “to my God,” which also the Prophets do, so making that which is common to all their own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what is there wonderful in the Prophets doing so?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For God himself plainly does it continually to His servants, calling Himself the God of Abraham and Isaac and Jacob, as peculiarly theirs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “That your faith is spoken of throughout the whole world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What then, had the whole world heard of the faith of the Romans?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yes, the whole, according to him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (Or, since that time, πἅσα ἐξ ἐκείνου).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And it is not a thing unlikely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the city was not one of no note, but as being upon a sort of eminence it was on every account conspicuous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But consider, I pray, the power of the preaching, how in a short time by means of publicans and fishermen it took hold upon the very head of all cities, and Syrians became the teachers and guides of Romans.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He attests then two excellencies in them, both that they believed, and that they believed with boldness, and that so great as that the fame of them reached into all the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For your faith,” he says “is spoken of throughout the whole world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Your faith,” not your verbal disputations, nor your questionings, nor your syllogisms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet there were there many hindrances to the teaching.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For having recently acquired the empire of the world they were elated, and lived in riches and luxury, and fishermen brought the preaching there, and they Jews and of the Jews, a nation hated and had in abomination among all men; and they were bidden to worship the Crucified, Who was brought up in Judea.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And with the doctrine the teachers proclaimed also an austere life to men who were practised in softness, and were agitated about things present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And they that proclaimed it were poor and common men, of no family, and born of men of no family.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But none of these things hindered the course of the word.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So great was the power of the Crucified as to carry the word round everywhere.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For it is spoken of,” he says, “in all the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He says not, it is manifested, but, is spoken of, as if all men had them in their mouths.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And indeed when he bears witness of this in the Thessalonians, he adds another thing also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For after saying, “from you sounded out the word of God,” he adds, “so that we need not to speak anything.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (1 Thess. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the disciples had come into the place of teachers, by their boldness of speech instructing all, and drawing them to themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the preaching came not anywhere to a stand, but went over the whole world more rapidly than fire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But here there is only thus much—“it is spoken of.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He well says that “it is spoken of,” showing that there was no need to add aught to what was said, or to take away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For a messenger’s business is this, to convey from one to another only what is told him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For which cause also the priest is called a “messenger” (Mal. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 7), because he speaks not his own words, but those of Him that sent him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet Peter had preached there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But he reckons what was his, to be his own as well.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: In such degree, as I said before, was he beyond measure clear of all grudging!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For God is my witness, whom I serve with my spirit in the Gospel of His Son.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Words these of an Apostle’s bowels of affection, the showing forth this of fatherly concernment!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what is it which he says, and why does he call God to witness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He had to declare his feeling toward them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since then he had not as yet ever seen them, he therefore called no man to witness, but Him Who entereth in the hearts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since he was saying, “I love you,” and as a token thereof alleged his praying continually for them, and wishing to come to them, and neither was this self-evident, he betakes himself to the trustworthy testimony.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Will then any one of you be able to boast that he remembers, when praying at his house (ἐπὶ τἥς οἰκίας) the entire body of the Church?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: I think not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But Paul drew near to God in behalf not of one city only, but of the whole world, and this not once, or twice, or thrice, but continually.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if the continually bearing any one about in one’s memory would not happen without much love; to have any in one’s prayers, and to have them there continually, think what great affection and friendship that implies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when he says, “Whom I serve with my spirit in the Gospel of His Son,” he shows us at once the grace of God, and also his own humble-mindedness; the grace of God because He entrusted to him so great a matter; but his own humility, because he imputes it all not to his own zeal, but to the assistance of the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the addition of “the Gospel,” shows the kind of ministry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For there are many and diverse modes of service.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And as under kings all are ranged under one that beareth kingly power, and all have not to minister (διακονοὕνται) about the same thing, but to one belongeth the ministry of ruling armies and to another that of ordering cities and to another again that of keeping treasures in the storehouses, thus also in spiritual things, one serveth God and laboreth (λατρεύει καὶδουλεύει) in believing and ordering his own life well, and another in undertaking the care of strangers, and another in taking in hand the patronship of them that be in need.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As even during the Apostle’s own time, they of Stephen’s company served God in the guardianship of the widows, others (ἄλλοι2 mss., all ὡν) in the teaching of the word, of whom also Paul was serving in the preaching of the Gospel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this was the fashion of his service: for it was to this that he was appointed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: On this account, he not only calls God to witness, but also says what he was entrusted with, to show that having so great things put into his hands, he would not have called Him Who trusted them to him to witness what was false.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And therewith he wished to make another point out also, viz. that he could not but have this love and care for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For that they might not say “who art thou? and, from whence? that thou sayest that thou art anxious over a city so great, and most imperial,” he shows that he must needs have this care, if at least the sort of service that was committed to him, was to declare the Gospel: for he that hath this put into his hands, must needs have continually upon his mind them that are to receive the word.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he shows another thing besides this by saying, “in my spirit;” that this service is much higher than either the Gentile or the Jewish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the Gentile is both fleshly and in error, and the Jewish is true indeed, yet even this is fleshly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But that of the Church is the opposite of the Gentile, but more lofty than the Jewish by a great deal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the mode of our service is not with sheep and oxen and smoke and fat, but by a spiritual soul, which Christ also shows in saying that “God is a Spirit, and they that worship Him must worship Him in spirit and in truth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “In the Gospel of His Son.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Having said above that it was the Father’s Gospel, here he says it is the Son’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So indifferent is it to say the Father’s or the Son’s!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he had learnt from that blessed voice that the things of the Father are the Son’s, and the things of the Son are the Father’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For “all Mine are Thine, and Thine are Mine.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “That without ceasing I make mention of you always in my prayers.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This is the part of genuine love, and he seems indeed to be saying some one thing, yet states four things even here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Both that he remembers, and that he does so continually, and that it is in his prayers, and that it is to ask great things for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Making request, if by any means now at length I might have a prosperous journey by the will of God to come unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see him painfully desiring to see them, and yet not enduring to see them contrary to what seemed good unto God, but having his longing mingled with the fear of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he loved them, and was eager to come to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet he did not, because he loved them, desire to see them, contrary to what seemed good unto God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This is true love, not as we love who err on both sides from the laws of love: for either we love no one, or if we ever do love, we love contrary to what seemeth good unto God, acting in both against the Divine law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And if these things be grievous (φορτικὰ) when spoken of, they are more grievous when done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And how do we love contrary to what seems good to God? (you will say.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: When we neglect Christ pining with hunger, and provide our children and friends and relations above their needs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or rather what need to carry the subject further.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if any one will examine his own conscience, he will find that this takes place in many things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But such was not that blessed person, but he knew both how to love and to love as he ought (3 mss. omit “as he ought”), and as was fitting, and though exceeding all men in loving, he transgressed not the measures of love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See then two things thrive extremely in him, fear of God, and also longing towards the Romans.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For to be praying continually, and not to desist when he obtained not, shows exceeding love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But while loving, thus to continue yielding to the will of God, shows intense reverence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: In another place, however, having “thrice besought the Lord” (2 Cor. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 8), he not only did not receive, but on the contrary, when he did not receive, he was very thankful for not having been heard.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So, in all things did he look to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But here he received, though not when he asked, but after delay, and neither hereat was he discontented.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And these things I mention that we may not repine at not being heard, or at being heard slowly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For we are not better than Paul, who confesses his thankfulness for both, and with good ground.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when he had once given himself up to the all-governing Hand, and put himself with as much subjection under it, as clay under the potter, he followed wheresoever God led.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Having then said that he desired to see them, he mentioned also the cause of his desire; and what is it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “That I may impart unto you some spiritual gift, to the end ye may be established.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it was not merely as many now go travelling in a needless and profitless way that he also went, but for necessary and very urgent ends.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he does not tell them his meaning openly, but by way of hints, for he does not say that I may teach you, that I may instruct you, that I may fill up that which is wanting; but, “that I may impart;” showing, that it is not his own things which he is giving them, but that he was imparting to them what he had received.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And here again he is unassuming, in saying “some,” he means, a small one, and suited to my powers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what may this small one be which thou art now going to impart?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This it is, he says, “to the end that ye may be established.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This then also cometh of grace, namely, the being unwavering and standing fast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when you hear of grace, think not that the reward of resolve on our part is thereby cast aside; for he speaks of grace, not to disparage the labor of resolve on our part, but to undermine (ὑποτεμνόμενος, as piercing a thing inflated) the haughtiness of an insolent spirit (ἀπονοίας).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Do not thou then, because that Paul hath called this a gift of grace, grow supine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he knows how, in his great candor, to call even well doings, graces; because even in these we need much influence from above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But in saying, “to the end that ye may be established,” he covertly shows that they needed much correction: for what he would say is this: Of a “long time I have both desired” and prayed to see you, for no other reason than that I may “stablish, strengthen, fix” you thoroughly in the word of God, so that ye be not continually wavering.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But he does not express himself so (for he would have shocked them), but in another way he hints to them the same thing, though in a subdued tone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when he says, “to the end that ye may be established,” he makes this plain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then since this also was very irksome, see how he softens it by the sequel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For that they may not say, are we wavering, and carried about? and need we speech of yours in order to stand fast? he anticipates and does away any gainsaying of the kind, by saying as follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “That is, that I may be comforted together with you by the mutual faith both of you and me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As if he said, Do not suspect that I spoke to accuse you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It was not with this feeling that I said what I did.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But what may it be that I wished to say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Ye are undergoing many tribulations, being drenched on every side (by those who persecute you περιαντλούμενοι.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 3 mss. παρενοχλούμενοι, harassed).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: I desired then to see you, that I might comfort you, or rather, not that I might comfort you only, but that I might myself receive comfort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See the wisdom of the teacher.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He said, to the end that “ye may be strengthened;” he knew that what he had said would be heavy and irksome to the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He says, “to the end that ye may be comforted.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But this again is heavy, not indeed to such a degree as the former, still it is heavy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He then pares down what is galling in this also, smoothing his speech on every side, and rendering it easy of acceptance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he does not say barely, “to be comforted,” but, “to be comforted together with you;” nor was he content with this but he puts in a further lenitive, when he says, “by the mutual faith both of you and me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Oh how great was his humble-mindedness!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He showed himself also to be in need of them, and not them only of him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he puts the disciples in the position of teachers, not letting any superiority remain upon his own side, but pointing out their full equality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the gain is mutual, he means, and I need the comfort from you, and you that from me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And how comes this to pass?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Through the mutual faith both of you and me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as in the case of fire, if any one gather together many lights, it is a bright flame that he kindles, thus also does it naturally happen with the faithful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when we be by ourselves, torn away from others, we are somehow in worse spirits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when we see one another, and are entwined with the members of our own selves, great is the comfort we receive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You must not look to the present time, during which, by God’s grace, both in city and in the desert itself, there be many hosts of believers, and all impiety hath been driven out; but consider, in that time, how great a good it was both for disciples to see their master, and for brethren who had come from another city to be seen of brethren.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But that I may make what I am saying plainer, let me bring the matter to an example.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if it should even happen and come to pass (may it never do so!) that we had been carried away to the land of the Persians or Scythians or other barbarians, and had been scattered (7 mss. “torn asunder”) by twos and threes in their cities, and were then suddenly to see any one of those here coming to us, reflect what a harvest of comfort we should reap of it!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See ye not those too who are in the prisons, it they see any of their acquaintance, how they revive, and are quite fluttering with the pleasure?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if I compare those days with captivity and imprisonment, count it no wonder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For these suffered far harder things than those, scattered as they were, and driven about, and dwelling in the midst of famine and of wars, and tremblingly expecting daily death, and suspecting friends and kindred and relatives, and dwelling in the world as in a strange land, aye, and in far harder plight than they who live in another’s country.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This is why he says, “to the end that ye may be established and comforted with us by our mutual faith.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this he says, not as though himself needed any assistance from them (far from it; for how should the pillar of the Church, who was stronger than iron and the rock, the spiritual adamant, who was equal to the charge of countless cities), but that he should not make his language impetuous and his reproof vehement, he says, that he himself also needs their consolation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if any one here should say, that the comfort was his gladness at the increase of their faith, and that Paul needed this, he would not be mistaking his meaning in this way either.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If then thou desire, one might say, and pray, and wilt gain comfort and give comfort by it, what is there to hinder thy coming?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: By way of dissipating this suspicion then, he proceeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Now I would not have you ignorant, brethren, that oftentimes I desired to come unto you (but was let hitherto).”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Here is a compliance great as that of slaves, and a plain exhibition of his excellent temper (εὐγνωμοσύνης)!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For, that he was let, he says, but why, he does not go on to say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he does not pry into the command of his Master, but only obeys.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet one might expect a person to start questions, as to why God hindered a city so conspicuous and great, and towards which the whole world was looking, from enjoying such a teacher, and that for so long a time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he that had overcome the governing city, could easily go on to the subjects of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But he that let alone the more royal one, and lay in wait about the dependents, had the main point left neglected.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But none of these things does he busy himself with, but yields to the incomprehensibleness of Providence, thereby both showing the right tone of his soul, and instructing us all never to call God to account for what happens, even though what is done seem to trouble the minds of many.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the Master’s part it is alone to enjoin, the servants’ to obey.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is why he says, that he was let, but not for what cause; for he means, even I do not know; ask not then of me the counsel or mind of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For neither “shall the thing formed say to him that formed it, Why hast thou made me thus?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For why, tell me, do you even seek to learn it? do you not know that all things are under His care, that He is wise, that He doeth nothing at a mere hazard, that He loveth thee more than they who begat thee, and goes exceeding far beyond a father’s yearnings of affection to thee, and a mother’s anxiousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Seek then no more, and go not a step further; for this is sufficient consolation for thee: since even then it was well ordered for the Romans.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And if thou knowest not the manner, take it not to heart: for this is a main feature of faith, even when in ignorance of the manner of the dispensation, to receive what is told us of His Providence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Paul then having succeeded in what he was earnest about (and what was this? to show that it was not as slighting them that he did not come to them, but because, though greatly desiring it, he was hindered), and having divested himself of the accusation of remissness, and having persuaded them that he was not less desirous to see them than themselves, further shows his love to them by other things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For even when I was hindered he means, I did not stand aloof from the attempt, but I kept attempting always yet was always hindered, yet never did I stand aloof thus, without falling out with the will of God, still keeping my love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For by his purposing it to himself and not standing aloof from it, he showed his affection; but through his being hindered and yet not struggling against it, all his love to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “That I might have some fruit among you also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet he had told them the cause of his longing before, and shown that it was becoming him; but still here also, he states it, clearing away all their suspicion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since the city was conspicuous, and in the whole extent of sea and land had no equal to many even the mere desire of becoming acquainted with it became a reason (πρόφασις) for a journey to it; that they might not think anything of the sort about Paul, or suspect that, merely with a view to glory in claiming them to himself he desired to be present there, he repeatedly lays down the ground of his desire, and before he says, it was that “I may impart to you some spiritual gift,” that I desired to see you; but here more clearly, “that I might have some fruit among you also even as among other Gentiles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The rulers he puts with the subjects, and after the countless triumphs and victories and the glory of the consuls, he puts them with the barbarians, and with good reason too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For where the nobility of faith is, there is none barbarian, none Grecian, none stranger, none citizen, but all mount up to one height of dignity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And see him here also unassuming, for he does not say, that I may teach and instruct, but what? “that I might have some fruit.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And not fruit, simply, but “some fruit.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Again, depreciating his own share therein just as he had said above, “that I may impart some gift.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And then to repress them too, as I said also before, he says, “even as among other Gentiles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For, I do not, because you are rich, and have the advantage of others, show less concern about the others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is not the rich that we are seeking, but the faithful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Where now are the wise of the Greeks, they that wear long beards and that are clad in open dress, and puff forth great words (τὰ μεγάλα φυσὥντες)?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: All Greece and all barbarian lands has the tentmaker converted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But Plato, who is so cried up and carried about among them, coming a third time to Sicily with the bombast of those words of his, with his brilliant reputation (ὑπσλήψεως), did not even get the better of a single king, but came off so wretchedly, as even to have lost his liberty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But this tentmaker ran over not Sicily alone or Italy, but the whole world; and while preaching too he desisted not from his art, but even then sewed skins, and superintended the workshop.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And even this did not give offence to those who were born of consuls, and with very good reason, for it is not their trades and occupations, but falsehood and forged doctrines, which usually render teachers easy subjects of contempt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And for this reason, even Athenians still laugh at the former.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But this man even barbarians attend to, and even foolish and ignorant men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For his preaching is set forth to all alike, it knows no distinction of rank, no preëminence of nation, no other thing of the sort; for faith alone does it require, and not reasonings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore it is most worthy of admiration, not only because it is profitable and saving, but that it is readily admissible and easy (Sav. “lovable”), and comprehensible to all: which is a main object in the Providence of God, who setteth forth His blessings to all in common.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what He did in respect of the sun and the moon and the earth and the sea and other things, not giving the rich and the wise a greater share of the benefits of these, and a less to the poor, but setting forth the enjoyment of them to all alike, this also did He with regard to the preaching, and even in a much greater degree, by how much this is more indispensable than they.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore Paul repeatedly says, “among all the Gentiles,” to show that he in no respect favors them, but is fulfilling his Master’s command, and sending them away to thanksgiving to the God of all, he says;

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “I am a debtor to the Greeks and to the Barbarians, both to the wise and to the unwise.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Which also he said when writing to the Corinthians.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he says it, to ascribe the whole to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “So, as much as in me is, I am ready to preach the Gospel to you that are at Rome also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Oh, noble soul! having taken on him a task laden of so great dangers, a voyage across the sea, temptations, plottings, risings—for it was likely, that one who was going to address so great a city which was under the tyrannic sway of impiety, should undergo temptations thick as snowflakes; and it was in this way that he lost his life in this city, being cut off by the tyrant of it—yet still expecting to undergo so great troubles, for none of these did he become less energetic, but was in haste and was in travail and was ready-minded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore he says, “So, as much as in me is, I am ready to preach the Gospel to you that are at Rome also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For I am not ashamed of the Gospel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: "What sayest thou, O, Paul?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: When it were fitting to say, that I boast, and am proud, and luxuriate in it; thou sayest not this, but what is less than this, that thou art “not ashamed,” which is not what we usually say of things very glorious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What then is this which he says, and why does he thus speak? while yet he exults over it more than over heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: At least, in writing to the Galatians, he said, “God forbid that I should glory, save in the Cross of our Lord Jesus Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How then comes he here to say, not that I even glory, but that “I am not ashamed?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The Romans were most anxiously eager about the things of the world, owing to their riches, their empire, their victories; and their kings they reckoned to be equal to the gods, and so they even called them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And for this cause too, they worshipped them with temples and with altars and with sacrifices.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since then they were thus puffed up, but Paul was going to preach Jesus, who was thought to be the carpenter’s son, who was brought up in Judea, and that in the house of a mean woman, who had no body guards, who was not encircled in wealth, but even died as a culprit with robbers, and endured many other inglorious things; and it was likely that they were concealing themselves as not as yet knowing any of the unspeakable and great things: for this reason he says, “I am not ashamed,” having still to teach them not to be ashamed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he knew that if they succeeded in this, they would speedily go on and come to glorying also: and do you then, if you hear any one saying, Dost thou worship the Crucified? be not ashamed, and do not look down, but luxuriate in it, be bright-faced at it, and with the eyes of a free man, and with uplifted look, take up your confession; and if he say again, Dost thou worship the Crucified? say in reply to him, Yes! and not the adulterer, not the insulter of his father, not the murderer of his children (for such be all the gods they have), but Him who by the Cross stopped the mouths of devils, and did away with their countless juggleries.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the Cross is for our sakes, being the work of unspeakable Love towards man, the sign of His great concern for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And in addition to what has been said, since they were puffed up with great pomposity of speech and with their cloak of external wisdom, I, he means to say, bidding an entire farewell to these reasonings, come to preach the Cross, and am not ashamed because of it: “for it is the power of God to salvation.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since there is a power of God to chastisement also (for when He chastised the Egyptians, He said, “This is My great power,”) (Joel ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 25) and a power to destruction, (for, “fear Him,” He says, “that is able to destroy both body and soul in hell”), (Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 28) for this cause he says, it is not these that I come to bring, the powers of chastisement and punishment, but those of salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Did not the Gospel tell of these things also, namely, the account of hell, and that of the outer darkness, and of the venomous worm?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet we know of these from no other source than the Gospel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: In what sense then does he say, “the power of God unto salvation?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Attend only to what follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “To every one that believeth; to the Jew first, and also to the Greek.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is not to all absolutely, but to them that receive it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For though thou be a Grecian (i.e. Heathen), and even one that has run into every kind of vice, though a Scythian, though a barbarian, though a very brute, and full of all irrationality, and burdened with the weights of endless sins, no sooner hast thou received the word concerning the Cross, and been baptized, than thou hast blotted out all these; and why says he here, “to the Jew first, and also to the Greek?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What meaneth this difference? and yet he has often said, “Neither circumcision is anything, nor uncircumcision” (1 Cor. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 15); how then doth he here discriminate, setting the Jew before the Greek?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now why is this? seeing that by being first he does not therefore receive any more of the grace (for the same gift is bestowed both on this person and that,) but the “first” is an honor in order of time only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he has no such advantage as that of receiving greater righteousness, but is only honored in respect of his receiving it first.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since in the case of those that are enlightened (you that are initiated know what is meant,) all run to the baptism, yet not all at the same hour, but one first and another second.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet the first doth not receive more than the second, nor he than the person after him, but all enjoy the same gifts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The “first” then here is an honor in word, not a superiority in grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then after saying, “unto salvation,” he enhances the gift further, by showing that it stayeth not at the present point, but proceedeth farther.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this is what he sets forth, when he says,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For therein is the righteousness of God revealed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But he who hath become just shall live, not for the present life only, but for that which is to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he hints not only this, but also another thing along with this, namely, the brightness and gloriousness of such a life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since it is possible to be saved, yet not without shame (as many are saved of those, who by the royal humanity are released from punishment), that no one may suspect this upon hearing of safety, he adds also righteousness; and righteousness, not thine own, but that of God; hinting also the abundance of it and the facility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For you do not achieve it by toilings and labors, but you receive it by a gift from above, contributing one thing only from your own store, “believing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then since his statement did not seem credible, if the adulterer and effeminate person, and robber of graves, and magician, is not only to be suddenly freed from punishment but to become just, and just too with the highest righteousness; he confirms his assertion from the Old Testament.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And first with a short sentence, he lays open a vast sea of histories to one who has a capacity for seeing them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For after having said, “from faith to faith,” he sends the hearer back to the dispensations of God, which took place thus in the Old Testament, which, when writing to the Hebrews, he explains with his usual great wisdom, showing that both the just and the sinners were justified in that way even then, wherefore also he made mention both of the harlot and of Abraham.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But then here, after having just hinted at it (for he was running on to another and a pressing subject), he again confirms what he had said from the Prophets, bringing in Habakkuk before them, crying, and saying, that it is not in the nature of things for him who is to live, to live otherwise save by faith; for “the just,” he says, “shall live by faith” (Hab. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 4), speaking about the life to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since what God giveth transcends reasoning entirely, it is but reason that we need faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the man that thinks meanly of it, and is contemptuous and vainglorious, will not effect anything at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Let heretics hearken to the voice of the Spirit, for such is the nature of reasonings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: They are like some labyrinth or puzzles which have no end to them anywhere, and do not let the reason stand upon the rock, and have their very origin in vanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For being ashamed to allow of faith, and to seem ignorant of heavenly things, they involve themselves in the dust-cloud of countless reasonings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then oh miserable and painful man, fit object for endless tears, should any one ask thee, how the heaven was made, and how the earth,—and why do I say the heaven and the earth? how thou wert thyself born, how nourished, and how thou grewest, art thou then not ashamed of thine ignorance?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if anything be said about the Only-begotten, dost thou thrust thyself through shame into a pit of destruction, thinking that it is unworthy of thee not to know everything?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet disputatiousness is an unworthy thing, and so is ill-timed curiosity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And why do I speak of doctrines? for even from the corruption in our present life we have escaped by no other means than through the faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Thus shone also all those aforetime, thus Abraham, thus Isaac, thus Jacob, thus too the harlot was saved, the one in the Old Testament, and likewise the one in the New.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For, “by faith,” he says, “the harlot Rahab perished not with them that believed not when she had received the spies.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if she had said to herself, “and how can they that are captives and exiles, and refugees, and live the life of vagabond tribes, get the better of us who have a city, and walls, and towers?” she would have destroyed both herself and them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Which also the forefathers of those who were then saved did suffer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when, upon the sight of men great and tall, they questioned the manner of victory, they perished, without battle or array, all of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Seest thou what a pit is that of unbelief! what a wall that of faith!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the one carried down endless thousands, the other not only saved a harlot, but made her the patroness of so numerous a people!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now since we know of these and more than these, never let us call God to account for what is done, but whatsoever He may lay on us, that let us take up with, and let us not run into niceties and curious questions, though to human reasoning the thing commanded appears even amiss.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what, let me ask, looks more amiss than for a father to slay with his own hands his only and legitimate son?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But still when the righteous man was bid do it, he raised no nice scruples about it, but owing to the dignity of the bidder, he merely accepted the injunction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And another too that was bidden of God to strike a prophet, when he raised nice scruples about the seeming unreasonableness of the injunction, and did not simply obey, he was punished to the extreme.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (1 Kings xx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But he that struck, gained a good report.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And Saul too, when he saved men contrary to the decree of God, fell from the kingdom, and was irretrievably punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And one might find other instances beside these: by all which we learn, never to require a reason for God’s injunctions, but to yield and obey only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if it be dangerous to raise nice scruples about aught that He may enjoin, and extreme punishment is appointed for those who are curious questioners, what possible excuse shall they have who curiously question things far more secret and awful than these, as for instance, how He begat the Son, and in what fashion, and what His Essence is?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now as we know this, let us with all kindliness receive the mother of all blessings, faith; that sailing as it were in a still harbor, we may at once keep our doctrines orthodox, and by steering our life safely in a straight course, may attain those eternal blessings by the grace and love toward man of our Lord Jesus Christ, through Whom and with Whom be glory unto the Father, with the Holy Ghost, for ever and ever.
